checks.finding: No unjustified inline decisions found. The two most novel technical decisions in the Assumptions section (cross-model-verify reuse for a domain-scoped bundle; c4-container template reuse) each cite a 'Technical-default basis' with exact file:line source evidence (prepare-panelist-input.sh:27-59, check-cross-model.sh:35, c4-container.template.md:1-21). Components table technology choices (PowerShell+bash, JSON Schema) align with rationale stated elsewhere (frontend-spec.md Technology Stack 'matches existing test suites'; Data Plan's design-tokens.json precedent for the JSON-contract pattern).

design.md ## Open Questions has OQ-001 and OQ-002, each with Owner: human, Blocks Implementation: no, and a stated Resolution Path ('confirm at task approval; revisit after first retrospective with drift metrics' / 'confirm at task approval; revisit via WFI'). Both required fields are present for both entries.

specs/sdd-domain/investigation.md is absent, but design.md's ## Assumptions section contains two non-trivial entries and both are independently grounded under basis 2 (reasonable technical default, basis cited): the cross-model-verify bundle assumption cites prepare-panelist-input.sh:27-59/91-95 and check-cross-model.sh:35 with line-level detail, and the c4-container template-reuse assumption cites c4-container.template.md:1-21. Since each non-trivial assumption states its own verified basis rather than being left ungrounded, the investigation.md-absence fallback trigger (bare, unsupported assumptions) does not apply here.

design.md has a ## Constraint Compliance section (lines 153-162) covering all constraints identifiable in requirements.md: visibility contract (AC-001/011), unidirectional flow (Main Workflows), graceful degradation / byte-identical absence behavior (AC-010), warn-first gates with two-release escalation (AC-009/R-3), R-10 protected-file procedure (R-1), and human-owned approval judgment (AC-007). The corrected Security Boundaries table in design.md now includes the external-vendor-egress (B4) boundary consistent with security-spec.md's B4 row; requirements.md's summary Security Boundaries table lists only 3 boundaries but is a non-exhaustive summary (explicitly delegates 'Details' to security-spec.md#trust-boundaries) and its Assumptions section already presupposes cross-model vendor reuse, so this is additive detail, not a contradiction.

No performance requirements found in requirements.md. A search for latency/throughput/response-time/SLA/concurrency/numeric-threshold keywords found only a qualitative cost/latency risk note (R-2: 'Cross-model verification at the domain gate adds cost/latency per model revision'), which is a risk acknowledgment, not a numeric performance requirement or SLA.

design.md ## Deployment / CI Plan (lines 146-151) states deployment target (no deployed service; reasoned N/A, local + CI only), CI pipeline changes (validate-repository.ps1 updated expectations plus new Pester suites), and the existing marketplace manifest pattern. Feature-flag strategy is inherent to the design (absence of domain/ directory disables the entire lane, stated in Constraint Compliance 'Graceful degradation' and Architecture note) rather than a separate flag mechanism. Data Plan states no schema changes and 'Migration Strategy: none required,' so migration-execution-order is not applicable.

design.md ## Data Plan states 'Existing Data Affected: none' and 'Migration Strategy: none required,' equivalent to the check's stated 'No data changes' PASS condition. No column/table schema changes are declared.

The sole external integration (cross-model panel of GPT/Gemini vendor slots, via the reused cross-model-verify skill) is identified in the Components table (name + purpose: 'Blind multi-vendor verdict on the reviewed model'), the Security Boundaries table (contract: 'Existing vendor API auth (reused unchanged); input-manifest allowlist limits the bundle to domain/*'), and failure behavior is explicit in Risks/Assumptions/requirements.md AC-006/AC-017 ('panelist-unavailable' recorded, requires_human_decision set, no auto-continue). GitHub Actions CI is pre-existing repository infrastructure, not a new third-party integration requiring this level of table detail.

All design.md components (domain-model, domain-interviewer, domain-reverse, domain-review-loop, domain-sync, domain-reviewer-a/b, check-domain-conformance, domain-contract schema, hook guard extension) map to requirements.md REQ-001..REQ-011 and US-1..US-4; no component, data model, or endpoint lacks a corresponding requirement. All four User Stories (US-1 interview, US-2 independent+cross-model review, US-3 bootstrap consumption, US-4 reverse-generation) and all Goals are covered by a named component in the Architecture/Components sections.

High-risk surfaces present: authorization/self-approval guard (B2) and cross-service integration failure behavior (B4, cross-model panel). Both have concrete named verification artifacts: TEST-007 and TEST-014 (tests/hooks/domain-approval-guard.Tests.ps1, per security-spec.md Security Tests) for the approval guard and post-approval reset, and TEST-006/TEST-017 (tests/sdd-domain/cross-model-gate.Tests.ps1) for cross-model mismatch and panelist-unavailable, all cross-referenced in design.md's Cross-Layer Dependencies and Test Strategy sections.
